texto: A picture in a book, a lynching. The bland faces of men who watcha Christ go up in flames, smiling,as if he were a hookedfish, a felled antelope, some wild thing tied to boards and burned.His charred bodygives off light--a haloburns out of him. His face is scorched featureless; the hair matted to the scalp like feathers. One man stands with his hand on his hip,another with his armslung over the shoulder of a friend,as if this moment were large enoughto hold affection.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Toi Derricotte: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “A picture in a book, a lynching. The bland faces of men who watcha Christ go up in flames, smilin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, lynching, poetry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e poetry.
